summary: A multi-author scientific paper (co-authored by Julie E. Buring) reporting the post-trial follow-up results of the Physicians' Health Study II. The study concludes that long-term Vitamin E and C supplementation had no effect on the risk of total or site-specific cancers in men.

principles.statement: Cancer prevention trials require continued post-trial follow-up to accurately assess long-term treatment effects.
principles.rationale: Cancer development is a prolonged, multistage process. Latent effects of an intervention—whether beneficial or harmful—may not be detectable during the limited duration of a standard trial and can only emerge years after the intervention ends.
principles.source_quote: Because of the long developing period for many cancers, continued posttrial follow-up is necessary in cancer prevention trials to assess long-term treatment effects, either beneficial or harmful, that may emerge after the intervention ends.
